reign in that fraternity's history so please put your preconceived notions aside it was actually really beneficial experience for me I got a swanky management consulting job doing government I got to figured if I'd rather die I really am sorry I was like don't be a fancy I like did it all I was like okay if we like make jokes throughout it like it'll be okay but anyways I packed my bags and I've never crossed the country straight and so this was the first of many times that I threw away everything that I had like worked so hard for that everyone had told me to do and what I was supposed to you know I'm that I broke the path and so I agreed to pay $10,000 to work for someone who used to call himself seven-figure Sam anyone know who that is yeah so you can still join it's like okay I was like here's $10,000 I was like what do I get he's like you work at my gym it's like this is interesting but anyways I learned a ton and 12 weeks later I ended up starting my first gym oops there was move to California because that's where everyone says that dreams come true right sorry dad in the audience and reality is that I got job at 24 hour fitness right and then that turned into sales boot camp which I had gone to school for exercise science and physiology and I had done a minor nutrition all that stuff so it's like coming up to this point that's all I knew right but it only took me two weeks of making absolutely no sales and crying in the bathroom stall let me help them if I cannot sell them right and nobody actually cared about their biomechanics or how limited their elbow range of motion is turns out so I dedicated myself to being really good at sales and it turns out that two years of high volume high to get fitness sales is actually very useful for helping people and making money so now we go back to post Alex right so I met this guy we're going on all these dates and you know he has all these gyms and I think he's really smart cuz like he just anytime he talks I was like man I just want to hear what he has to say next right like he's so and I was like what does that even mean he's like I mean like I know where you're at right now like I was there a few years ago and like let me save you the hassle and just come do this with me and I'll teach you everything I know and I was like oh and I was at this point in my life where I was like I don't know what I want to to do that yet right doing it right there like they're taking like you're you're cutting grass with scissors and I was like okay and three weeks of us knowing each other for Alex to come up with this and I was like we're just gonna date like we're not gonna talk about work stuff like we don't need to do that and then I'm sure all of you have seen this video right where Alex signed up 191 people in 19 days at $500 each in the hood and so every night when he was there he would call me and normally it was us FaceTiming and then suddenly he would fall asleep on the FaceTime and the phone would just be staring at the ceiling he'd be like I closed 17 and he told me that he was like I know we haven't gone like a really nice date but I just need you to help me process this paperwork first I was like what the heck dude and he's like I'll show you how to use mine body like I have all these contracts so he came with a backpack full of contracts like this thing I had ever seen processed in my whole life I was like oh my gosh holy crap dude like my years of clients and reputation behind a week later like any normal person would and we spent the next 10 months together galvanizing around the country launching gyms and we actually lived out of the extended stay for almost a year so if you're ever gonna go to the extended stay get the room on the first floor right next to the office because my partner filed bankruptcy and so all the money from the challengers and all the money that I'd done from selling my gyms was gone so my whole life at that point I was gone so I remember screenjotted our only car so at 236 I call an Uber and I'm like oh you're two hours from us okay cool so we uber two hours to the police station and the next day was probably the hardest that we ever had together he's like the days working and grinding for a year flying out to all these new locations was exhausting but it all like come to this cumulative point which I think the next day was like it was really other way yeah and so my mother was actually also in the hospital trying to stay alive at this time so I saw eight weeks and so it was stressful also the ability which is also I'm familiar with very familiar with the processing world now so I couldn't even sell my way out of trying to fix the hole because I couldn't process payments because they shut me down and so they actually were holding on shut down and he shows up to the theater and my dad comes up to me he's like he seems a little stressed it was Christmas Eve yeah and we go into the movie theater and I just hear like are you okay and he's like I don't how would be fun I checked his heart rate it was literally over a hundred beats per minute while he was just sitting there in the movie theater it's a little scary yeah and so then at that point we were like you know what we're doing gym lunch and so we asked all of Layla's friends to quit their jobs and to be dependent on us which was a great decision and they did and they're might recognize things started to grow mind you we still have not figured out the processing situation yet so I've launched all these gyms and could not process the money that we were selling so we were just stacking contracts but could not process the money yet and then something else happened so we ended up resolving that processing issue and I had to like process them all like a month late and then half didn't go through it was a big pain in the ass but anyways three months in now like we started to gain a little bit of momentum and that we had signed up for them because they couldn't handle a hundred plus sign-ups so within seven days we had a hundred twenty thousand dollars of refunds that went out and super big in the original launch business because there's so much overhead and so like the small nest egg that we had had like disappeared in five days because it was they were really I mean they were refunds like they just got the model was fundamentally flawed because the gyms gym owners weren't invested and they didn't really like being dependent on some dude from the internet right like they're like yeah you can launch my gym like I don't really know who you are sure you can send somebody and we'd sign hundred people up and they weren't involved at all and so we were like you know what let's we know how to sell we know how to market like let's just take the gyms out the girl copy even though I'm not a girl she read this and she was like this is my story but it's like so sad and compelling started working and I was like you know what we could just we could bring all the sales guys in house they don't have to be on the field anymore like this could work we can turn this around and so I called up all the gyms that were scheduled for the next month that we're supposed to launch we were supposed to fly out and I was like hey guys like me so like I can they're like no man like you can't because like I need this and I was like yeah you know I just kind of got need this and I was like all right well I'll show you how to do nothing back the gym launch exploded and so I was like huh so I called up the other gyms that we had launched we launched like 28 of them at that point and I was like do you want to see how I made 50 grand from your facility in 21 days and they were like yeah I was like huh I was like will you pay me for it they're like yeah and I was like I think this is a better way to do it than like everyone wins like they learn how to do it they're not dependent on us sweet let's triple the ad spend right just me just meeting so if you guys got on and you had a call for the sales guy to buy gym launch in the first place I had four to three to four of you want to same time it was she's doing group high ticket closes so it was um it was crazy and it was at that point when everything was kind of coming and it was at that point that we looked at each other and we were like we need to get married but um this was a letter that I wrote to Alex and it kind of sums up how we felt that year and so I hope it if any of you are struggling or you have struggled in the past you know that like you're not uncomfortable years of my life we went through so many circumstances and situations that test us both as individual and as teammates when we first start off together I knew that you were not the person I wanted to end up with and I was not the person that you wanted to end up with but what I didn't know for sure at the time and as time went on was that we had the potential and desire to grow into those people you tell me I was crazy for being with you but I was crazy enough just to see what could block with each layer I saw a new level of beauty and you hadn't seen before I fell more in love with you as you let your walls down and in return I learned to let mine down walls I had been up and guarded for my heart for years walls I thought were keeping me safe but in reality we're keeping me isolated for the first time in years I let someone see me for all that I am the good the bad and ugly that I desperately wanted to hide and the years I had trouble trying to cover up and fix and you loved me anyways do you know how good that feels I'm so grateful for you I'm sure you've seen me at my worst by now living out of hotels eating out of gas stations stressing about our relationship and the business I look back and I am astounded that we didn't kill each other at times those are the circumstances that break so many people let alone relationships and that is the one reason I have so much people picture of our life together clearly no of course not it's a blur but isn't that what faith is taking the first step without seeing the whole staircase and that's how Jim launch was born and so because we kind of felt like we were on borrowed time you know I mean like we had lost everything and had said such a rough start because people were like how is your marriage so strong it's because we went through that was our dating period and so like once once the business turned if we're gonna do it let's do it our way you know so like what's all the stuff we hate about masterminds and gurus and agencies and all this crap like what do we hate about it it was like they never have any help there's no new knows how to help you with the tech stuff because that's where everyone gets like bogged down I don't know how to and like it's very clear with the like the person said the same issue for three hot seats in a row like you have issues that are not tactical like you have limiting beliefs and like I don't really want to sit here for 20 minutes to hear you talk about why you can't get your team on board you know I mean with X you know it's like I get it and so it just it just wasn't valuable for me like or the wanted to like ask you for your stuff and like like well I can't tell you what's going on in my gym you know like seriously it was like that where they would give you like false information they'd be like oh this is how we do our sales I'm like it makes sense like we're like you know you just got to just got to really love the team I'm like that's not tactical or useful thanks for that and then
